Name: Meganium
Ability-Effect: Each Basic [G] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon provides [G][G] Energy. The effect of Wild Growth doesn’t stack.
Flavor-Text: Anyone who stands beside it becomes refreshed, just as if they were soaking in the soothing atmosphere of a forest.

Name: Inteleon
Effects: Choose a Pokémon in play (yours or your opponent’s) that has the least HP remaining, except for this Pokémon, and it is Knocked Out.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses special lenses in its eyes to sense things about its opponents—like their body heat—then attacks once it identifies a weak spot.

Name: Alakazam
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Draw 3 cards.
Effects: Place 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each card in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Alakazam wields potent psychic powers. It’s said that this Pokémon used these powers to create the spoons it holds.

Name: Lunatone
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if you have Solrock in play, you may discard a Basic [F] Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Draw 3 cards. You can’t use more than 1 Lunar Cycle Ability each turn.
Flavor-Text: It was discovered at the site of a meteor strike 40 years ago. Its stare can lull its foes to sleep.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.

Name: Drifblim
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It can generate and release gas within its body. That’s how it can control the altitude of its drift.

Name: Psyduck
Ability-Effect: Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) lose any Ability that requires the Pokémon using it to Knock Out itself.
Flavor-Text: It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

Name: Golduck
Ability-Effect: Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) lose any Ability that requires the Pokémon using it to Knock Out itself.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it swims at full speed using its long, webbed limbs, its forehead somehow begins to glow.

Name: Alakazam
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Draw 3 cards.
Effects: Place 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each card in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Alakazam wields potent psychic powers. It’s said that this Pokémon used these powers to create the spoons it holds.

Name: Riolu
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Accelerating Stab.
Flavor-Text: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

Name: Mega Latias ex
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Lucario ex
Effects: Attach up to 3 Basic [F] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Mega Brave.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Venusaur ex
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may use this Ability. Move a Basic [G] Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Ceruledge
Effects: Discard 4 Basic [R] Energy cards from your hand. If you can’t discard 4 cards in this way, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The fiery blades on its arms burn fiercely with the lingering resentment of a sword wielder who fell before accomplishing their goal.

Name: Zacian
Effects: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Able to cut down anything with a single strike, it became known as the Fairy King’s Sword, and it inspired awe in friend and foe alike.

Name: Flygon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. You may also use this Ability if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon. Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Known as the Desert Spirit, this Pokémon hides in the sandstorms it causes by beating its wings.

Name: Toxtricity
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Basic [D] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched [D] Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As it scatters toxic sweat and emits electricity, a melody that sounds like it came from a guitar reverberates through the surrounding area.

Name: Cottonee
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Pillows and beds stuffed with cotton exhaled by Cottonee are soft and puffy, light and airy—altogether top quality.

Name: Whimsicott
Effects: Heal all damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Like the wind, it can slip through any gap, no matter how small. It leaves balls of white fluff behind.

Name: Sneasel
Flavor-Text: This cunning Pokémon hides under the cover of darkness, waiting to attack its prey.

Name: Weavile
Effects: If this Pokémon’s remaining HP is 50 or less, this attack does 170 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Evolution made it even more devious. It communicates by clawing signs in boulders.

Name: Charcadet
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

Name: Mega Charizard X ex
Effects: Discard any amount of [R] Energy from among your Pokémon, and this attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Oricorio ex
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, if you have any [R] Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Attach a Basic [R] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched [R] Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Kangaskhan ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may use this Ability. Draw 2 cards. You can’t use more than 1 Run Errand Ability each turn.
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Meloetta
Effects: Heal 120 damage from 1 of your Benched [P] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

Name: Haunter
Flavor-Text: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

Name: Celebratory Fanfare
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may heal 10 damage from each of their Pokémon. If a player healed any damage in this way, their turn ends.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Mega Charizard X ex
Effects: Discard any amount of [R] Energy from among your Pokémon, and this attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Charizard Y ex
Effects: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 280 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: N’s Zekrom
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use attacks.
Flavor-Text: When the interior part of its tail spins like a motor, Zekrom can generate many bolts of lightning to blast its surroundings.

Name: Mega Gardevoir ex
Effects: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic [P] Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 50 damage for each [P] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Lucario ex
Effects: Attach up to 3 Basic [F] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Mega Brave.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Meganium ex
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Emboar ex
Effects: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Feraligatr ex
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 200 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Bulbasaur
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

Name: Squirtle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.

Name: Turtwig
Flavor-Text: Made from soil, the shell on its back hardens when it drinks water. It lives along lakes.

Name: Chimchar
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It agilely scales sheer cliffs to live atop craggy mountains. Its fire is put out when it sleeps.

Name: Piplup
Flavor-Text: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

Name: Rowlet
Flavor-Text: This wary Pokémon uses photosynthesis to store up energy during the day, while becoming active at night.

Name: Litten
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

Name: Popplio
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon snorts body fluids from its nose, blowing balloons to smash into its foes. It’s famous for being a hard worker.

Name: Chikorita
Flavor-Text: A sweet aroma gently wafts from the leaf on its head. It is docile and loves to soak up sun rays.

Name: Cyndaquil
Flavor-Text: It is timid and always curls itself up in a ball. If attacked, it flares up its back for protection.

Name: Totodile
Flavor-Text: Its powerful, well-developed jaws are capable of crushing anything. Even its Trainer must be careful.

Name: Snivy
Flavor-Text: It is very intelligent and calm. Being exposed to lots of sunlight makes its movements swifter.

Name: Tepig
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It blows fire through its nose. When it catches a cold, the fire becomes pitch-black smoke instead.

Name: Oshawott
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

Name: Grookey
Flavor-Text: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area

Name: Scorbunny
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: A warm-up of running around gets fire energy coursing through this Pokémon's body. Once that happens, it's ready to fight at full power.

Name: Sobble
Flavor-Text: When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won't be able to resist weeping.

Name: Serperior
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play.
If Rosa’s Encouragement is in your discard pile, this attack does 150 more damage.
Flavor-Text: An intimidating gaze and majestic appearance have caused this Pokémon to be known as the Lord of the Forest.

Name: Barbaracle
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Attach a Basic [F] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your [F] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Barbaracle’s legs and hands have minds of their own, and they will move independently. But they usually follow the head’s orders.

Name: Tyrantrum
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, it gets +150 HP.
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon behaved like a king 100 million years ago. Nothing could stop it.

Name: Doublade
Effects: Reveal any number of Honedge, Doublade, and Aegislash from your hand, and this attack does 60 damage for each card you revealed in this way.
Flavor-Text: When Honedge evolves, it divides into two swords that cooperate via telepathy to coordinate attacks and slash their enemies to ribbons.

Name: Makuhita
Flavor-Text: It grows stronger by enduring harsh training. It is a gutsy Pokémon that can withstand any attack.

Name: Chikorita
Flavor-Text: It loves to bask in the sunlight. It uses the leaf on its head to seek out warm places.

Name: Tyrunt
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. If something happens that it doesn’t like, it throws a tantrum and runs wild.

Name: Mega Zygarde ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
For each of your opponent’s Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 150 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Delphox
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may discard a Basic [R] Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to all Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using psychic power, it generates a fiery vortex of 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit, incinerating foes swept into this whirl of flame.

Name: Ampharos
Ability-Effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, attacks used by this Pokémon do 80 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Flashing Bolt.
Flavor-Text: The tail’s tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

Name: Crobat
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a card. Shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Both of its legs have turned into wings. Without a sound, Crobat flies swiftly toward its prey and sinks its fangs into the nape of its target’s neck.

Name: Goodra
Ability-Effect: When your opponent’s Active Pokémon retreats, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, Energy for its Retreat Cost is not discarded, and they don’t switch Pokémon. The effect of Slimy Sliding doesn’t stack.
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck.
Flavor-Text: This very friendly dragon Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime.

Name: Toxel
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It has no problem drinking dirty water. An organ inside Toxel’s body filters such water into a poisonous liquid that is harmless to Toxel.

Name: Charmeleon
Flavor-Text: When it swings its burning tail, the temperature around it rises higher and higher, tormenting its opponents.

Name: Fennekin
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.
